meta.purpose: First draft of IoTEdges self-developed product model tiers derived from mainstream industrial IoT product configurations and the IoTEdges first-generation product strategy.
meta.important_note: Model names and specs are engineering planning targets, not final datasheets. Certifications, LTE bands, IO counts, enclosure ratings, protocol support and software features must be validated through product design, prototype testing and documentation before publishing as final website claims.

connectivity_code_rule.note: A device can have only one wireless uplink method. 4G LTE, WiFi and LoRa/LoRaWAN must be separate device variants, not combined in the same device. Ethernet can still be used as a wired uplink or configuration port.

naming_policy.note: Model codes are planning names. Final SKU names can be changed after engineering definition and product validation.

product_families.positioning: Industrial gateways for Modbus, MQTT, Ethernet, RS485 and cloud/SCADA-style remote monitoring.

models.seo_role: Ethernet-only base product for Industrial IoT Gateway, MQTT Gateway, Modbus Gateway and Ethernet Industrial Gateway pages.

serial: optional RS232 if product design includes it

models.claim_policy: Can be marketed as the default wired industrial IoT gateway after Modbus/MQTT/Ethernet/RS485 behavior is implemented and validated.

models.seo_role: Cellular gateway for 4G MQTT Gateway, Cellular IoT Gateway and remote equipment monitoring pages.
models.claim_policy: Do not publish LTE band, country coverage, VPN or dual-SIM claims until module selection, firmware and prototype tests confirm them.

models.seo_role: WiFi gateway model for WiFi MQTT Gateway and indoor industrial IoT gateway pages.
models.claim_policy: Do not publish WiFi certification, antenna or deployment range claims until module selection, firmware and RF tests confirm them.

models.seo_role: Higher-tier model for Edge Controller, MQTT Edge Gateway, IIoT Edge Gateway and OEM remote monitoring pages.
models.claim_policy: Keep this model as a phase-2 product until edge software capability is genuinely defined and tested.

product_families.positioning: RTU devices for field IO, Modbus data, alarms and remote telemetry across water, energy, oil and gas, telecom and environmental monitoring.

models.seo_role: Ethernet-only base RTU model for Industrial RTU, Ethernet RTU, Remote IO for SCADA and local cabinet monitoring pages.

protocols: Modbus TCP or Ethernet data uplink if supported

models.claim_policy: Publish IO channel counts only after schematic design and prototype validation.

models.seo_role: Main 4G RTU model for remote pump, tank, pipeline, telecom, generator and environmental monitoring pages.
models.claim_policy: Do not publish LTE band or regional compatibility until module selection, firmware and prototype tests confirm them.

models.seo_role: WiFi RTU model for indoor facility monitoring, factory utility monitoring and building equipment alarm pages.
models.claim_policy: Do not publish WiFi certification, antenna or deployment range claims until module selection, firmware and RF tests confirm them.

models.seo_role: Higher channel-count RTU for wastewater treatment, substation cabinet, factory utility and multi-sensor sites.
models.claim_policy: Use as an expansion model after the base and 4G RTU are defined.

product_families.positioning: Distributed IO modules for collecting and controlling digital, analog and relay signals through Modbus/RS485 or Ethernet.

models.seo_role: Core wired Remote IO model for DI/DO/AI/AO collection, Modbus Remote IO, RS485 Remote IO and Ethernet Remote IO product pages.

protocols: Modbus TCP optional if Ethernet variant exists

not_in_baseline: independent RTU/cellular telemetry unless paired with gateway
models.claim_policy: Keep Remote IO claims focused on signal collection/control and Modbus integration.

models.seo_role: Wireless IO node for LoRa Remote IO, agriculture monitoring, environmental monitoring, street light monitoring and distributed sensor pages.

power: battery/solar option only after power budget validation
installation: field enclosure variant if outdoor use is required

models.claim_policy: Use as a phase-2 product until power budget, RF performance and enclosure design are validated.

product_families.positioning: LoRa/LoRaWAN gateways for long-range wireless monitoring in agriculture, environmental, water, cold chain and smart city applications.

models.seo_role: Core LoRa gateway model for agriculture, environmental and cold-chain wireless monitoring pages.
models.claim_policy: Do not publish range, frequency or outdoor rating claims until RF design, module choice and field tests confirm them.

recommended_first_product_lineup.reason: Lowest-risk core gateway for Modbus, MQTT, RS485 and Ethernet SEO pages.
High commercial value for 4G RTU, water, telecom, oil/gas and remote monitoring pages.
Supports Remote IO, Modbus IO, factory, water, building and SCADA content.
Useful 4G gateway variant for remote Modbus/MQTT sites.
Supports WiFi MQTT Gateway and indoor industrial gateway SEO pages without mixing WiFi into 4G hardware.
Supports WiFi RTU pages for indoor facilities, buildings and factory utility monitoring.
Expands agriculture, environmental, cold chain and smart city solution coverage.
Supports LoRa Remote IO and distributed wireless sensor solution pages after the core LoRa gateway is defined.

safe_to_say_now: IoTEdges is defining self-developed product families for industrial gateways, RTUs, Remote IO and LoRa gateways.
Product pages should describe use cases and target architectures at a category level until model specs are validated.
Planned model tiers include Ethernet-only 100-series wired baseline models, separate 4G variants, separate WiFi variants, separate LoRa/LoRaWAN variants and advanced/edge variants.
4G LTE, WiFi and LoRa/LoRaWAN are planned as separate wireless uplink variants, not combined in the same device.
5G products are not part of the first-generation IoTEdges roadmap.
do_not_say_until_confirmed: Exact LTE bands or country compatibility
Certifications such as CE, FCC, ATEX, IECEx or UL
OPC UA, BACnet, CAN, VPN, TLS or data logging unless confirmed
Specific cloud platform certification or compatibility
Guaranteed range, uptime, latency or data retention

next_actions: Use self-developed-product-spec-baseline.json as the source baseline for first-generation product requirements.
Convert selected models into engineering requirement documents before hardware and firmware development.
Replace planned fields with validated specs in this file and capability-check-matrix.json after prototype testing.
Only then start drafting final product Markdown pages.
